title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhed: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: Cannes 2026: Film influencers, the new stars of the red carpet
subhed: They are often young, sometimes with hundreds of thousands of followers on social media, and have completely reshaped the landscape of film promotion in the media. Filmmakers are increasingly relying on the influential power of these new ambassadors of the silver screen.

title: Hantavirus: How great is the transmission risk, as each country sets its own quarantine rules?
subhed: All passengers from the MV Hondius have now been repatriated to their countries of origin. This marks a critical phase, as follow-up and quarantine protocols are not standardized across borders.

title: Scientists warn 2026 could be extreme year as global warming slips down agenda
subhed: Experts and international agencies issued a fresh warning on Tuesday as the anticipated return of the natural El Niño phenomenon is expected to worsen an already accelerating climate crisis.

title: Inside the mutually beneficial relationship of shipping magnate Saadé and President Macron
subhed: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (1/4) The French leader and the head of the company CMA CGM have developed a relationship that is as respectful as it is useful for both parties, given how closely the interests of the family-owned group and the French state are intertwined.

title: 'There are no innocents in Gaza': Channel 14, the Israeli television channel accused of genocidal rhetoric
subhed: The channel, whose ratings have been steadily increasing, gives prominence to the most violent rhetoric, including calls for the 'annihilation' of the Palestinian territory. Three Israeli NGOs have compiled a detailed record of these statements, to which Le Monde has had access, and have filed a petition before the Supreme Court.
